name: Djinni
special_attacks: Air Mastery (+1 atk/dmg vs. flying creatures)
Whirlwind (once/10 min; 10-30 ft. tall; 5-15 ft. wide; 1d8x(HD/2) damage; DC 17 Ref)
Spell-Like Abilities (CL 9): invisibility, major creation, persistent image DC 17, plane shift, wind walk, create food and water, create wine, major creation 1/day
ecology_notes: Djinn are noble genies of the air plane, masters of wind and sky. They view themselves as superior to other genies and often appear in tales as wish-granters bound to service.

name: Efreeti
special_attacks: Heat (1d6 fire damage on contact)
Spell-Like Abilities (CL 12): detect magic, enlarge person, invisibility, pyrotechnics DC 14, plane shift, produce flame, scorching ray, wall of fire
Grant up to 3 wishes to non-efreet
ecology_notes: Efreet are the powerful and cruel genies of the Elemental Plane of Fire. They despise all other genies, especially djinn, and keep humanoid slaves in their brass cities.

name: Jann
special_attacks: Spell-Like Abilities (CL 6): create food and water, ethereal jaunt 1/day, invisibility 2/day, plane shift 1/day, speak with animals
special_qualities: Elemental Endurance (24 hrs on any elemental plane)
ecology_notes: Jann are the weakest of the genie-kin, born of all four elements. They are the only genies who make the Material Plane their primary home, often working as guides, mercenaries, or scouts.

name: Marid
special_attacks: Drench (extinguish non-magical flames by touch)
Spell-Like Abilities (CL 9): control water, control weather, plane shift, water breathing; create water 3/day, fog cloud 3/day, call lightning 1/day, invisibility 1/day, plane shift 1/day
ecology_notes: Marids are the greatest of the water genies, proud and capricious masters of the seas. They rule vast underwater realms and have power over water and weather.

name: Formian Warrior
special_attacks: Poison (DC 14 Fort; 1d6 Str primary / 1d6 Str secondary)
ecology_notes: Formian Warriors are the soldiers of the hive, equipped with venomous stingers and hardened carapaces. They fight in coordinated groups directed by the hive mind.

name: Formian Taskmaster
special_attacks: Dominate Monster (once/week)
Mind Control (compel 1 creature to fight as dominated; DC 18 Will)
Poison (DC 18 Fort; 2d6 Str primary / 2d6 Str secondary)
ecology_notes: Formian Taskmasters oversee the labor of enslaved workers and other creatures. They can dominate the minds of powerful creatures to force them into service.

name: Formian Myrmarch
special_attacks: Poison (DC 20 Fort; 2d6 Str primary / 2d6 Str secondary)
Spell-Like Abilities (CL 12): calm emotions, charm monster, detect chaos, detect thoughts, dictum, magic circle against chaos, order's wrath
ecology_notes: Formian Myrmarchs serve as generals and commanders of formian armies, guiding strategy and wielding powerful spell-like abilities in addition to formidable physical combat power.

name: Formian Queen
special_attacks: Hive Mind (500-mile radius)
Poison (DC 22 Fort; 2d8 Str primary / 2d8 Str secondary)

Spell-Like Abilities (CL 17): calm emotions, charm monster, detect chaos, detect thoughts, discern location, divination, dominate monster, feeblemind, hold monster, mass charm monster, order's wrath, status, telepathy
special_qualities: Hive Mind (connected to all formians within 50 miles)
ecology_notes: The Formian Queen is the center of the hive. She is immobile and focuses entirely on breeding, directing the hive mind, and protecting the colony. Each hive has exactly one queen.

name: Inevitable, Kolyarut
special_attacks: Enervation Ray (60 ft., +13 ranged touch, 1d4 negative levels, DC 18 Fort to remove after 24 hrs)
Spell-Like Abilities (CL 13): discern lies, fear, hold monster, lesser geas, locate creature, suggestion, true seeing
ecology_notes: Kolyaruts enforce contracts and oaths. They hunt those who break their word and will not stop until the contract-breaker has faced justice. They appear as amber-colored metal humanoids.

name: Inevitable, Marut
special_attacks: Fists of Thunder (on hit, DC 23 Fort or deafened 1 hour)
Fists of Lightning (secondary slam; DC 23 Ref or blinded)
Spell-Like Abilities (CL 14): chain lightning, circle of death, dictum, fear, greater dispel magic, mass inflict light wounds, true seeing, wall of force
ecology_notes: Maruts pursue those who have unnaturally extended their lives past their allotted span, hunting liches, vampires, and others who cheat death. They appear as towering, golden humanoids of imposing mass.

name: Inevitable, Zelekhut
special_attacks: Chains (trip attempt on hit, no AoO; reach 10 ft.)
Spell-Like Abilities (CL 8): clairaudience/clairvoyance, dimensional anchor, dispel magic, fear DC 17, hold monster DC 17, locate creature, mark of justice, true seeing
ecology_notes: Zelekhuts hunt escaped criminals and those who flee from justice. They appear as centaur-like constructs of gold and silver with great chained weapons.

name: Night Hag
special_attacks: Dream Haunting (enter dreams of sleeping victim; DC 16 Will or gain a negative level each night)
Spell-Like Abilities (CL 8): detect chaos/evil/good/law, magic missile, ray of enfeeblement, sleep; astral projection and etherealness at will
ecology_notes: Night Hags exist on the ethereal plane and haunt the dreams of evil mortals, slowly stealing their souls to sell to the highest bidder in the Lower Planes.

name: Nightmare
special_attacks: Smoke (riders must make DC 13 Fort each round or choke, -2 to atk/dmg; smoke fills 5-ft. radius around Nightmare)
ecology_notes: Nightmares are terrifying fiendish horses with flaming hooves and billowing smoke. They serve as mounts for powerful evil creatures and can travel between planes.

name: Slaad, Red
special_attacks: Implant (on critical hit; DC 17 Fort or slaad egg develops; after 90 days rips out as blue slaad tadpole)
ecology_notes: Red Slaadi are brutish frog-like creatures that implant their eggs in hosts. The least intelligent of the slaadi, they exist mainly to fight and breed.

name: Slaad, Blue
special_attacks: Disease (claws; DC 16 Fort or infected; after 1d6+6 weeks transforms into red slaad)
ecology_notes: Blue Slaadi are larger and more dangerous than red. Their claws carry a disease that transforms victims into red slaadi if not cured.

name: Slaad, Green
special_attacks: Spell-Like Abilities (CL 9): chaos hammer, detect thoughts, fear, passwall, shatter, telekinesis
ecology_notes: Green Slaadi are more intelligent than their lesser kin and can polymorph into humanoid form to infiltrate societies. They have powerful spell-like abilities and can command red and blue slaadi.

name: Slaad, Gray
special_attacks: Spell-Like Abilities (CL 14): animate dead, chaos hammer, circle of chaos, confusion, detect thoughts, dispel law, fireball, fly, identify, magic missile, passwall, shatter, telekinesis, true seeing
special_qualities: Alternate Form (humanoid or toad at will)
ecology_notes: Gray Slaadi are the most intelligent of the lesser slaadi. They serve as leaders and generals, with powerful magic and the ability to assume any humanoid form at will.

name: Slaad, Death
special_attacks: Spell-Like Abilities (CL 18): animate dead, chaos hammer, circle of chaos, confusion, cure critical wounds, detect magic, dispel law, fireball, fly, inflict critical wounds, magic missile, passwall, plane shift, shatter, telekinesis, true seeing, word of chaos
ecology_notes: Death Slaadi are the most powerful of their kind, barely distinguishable from powerful humanoids in their natural form. They are the true slaad lords and can draw on massive divine-like power.

name: Tojanida, Juvenile
special_attacks: Ink Cloud (10 ft. radius, blinds creatures 1 round)
ecology_notes: Tojanida are turtle-like outsiders native to the Elemental Plane of Water. Their hard shells and multiple eyes make them difficult to surprise or flank.

name: Tojanida, Adult
special_attacks: Ink Cloud (30 ft. radius, blinds creatures 1 round)
ecology_notes: Adult Tojanida are larger and stronger than juveniles, their shells nearly impenetrable. They are known to be cantankerous and territorial.

name: Tojanida, Elder
special_attacks: Ink Cloud (60 ft. radius, blinds creatures 1 round)
ecology_notes: Elder Tojanida are ancient and massive, with shells thick enough to deflect most weapons. They are among the oldest creatures of the Elemental Plane of Water.

name: Xorn, Minor
ecology_notes: Minor Xorn are the smallest of their kind, sifting through stone for metals and gems. They are not hostile unless threatened or denied their diet of precious minerals.

name: Xorn, Average
ecology_notes: Average Xorn are the most commonly encountered size, burrowing through the Elemental Plane of Earth in search of metals and gems for sustenance.

name: Xorn, Elder
ecology_notes: Elder Xorn are massive and powerful, capable of consuming entire veins of ore. They are among the most feared creatures of the Earth plane.
